data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism's per-question strict-beat condition requires r_w(q)*n_wrong(q) > n_wrong(q) - n_correct(q), but for odd k this right-hand side is negative whenever SC is wrong (n_correct > n_wrong for a wrong majority), making the inequality trivially satisfied by any nonzero flag rate on wrong candidates — the condition does not actually discriminate helpful from harmful critic behavior. More critically, the scope covers base error p in (0.4, 0.8), but the repository's measured data are at base error 0.033 (easy) and 0.01 (hard) — both far outside the claimed regime — so the prediction is unfalsifiable by the available evidence and the proposed experiment would need to manufacture an entirely different error distribution, potentially violating the same-pool constraint.
